message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Importance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Reopening of universities in the U.S. has been controversial in the setting of the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    To investigate (1) the association between new COVID-19 cases since September 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    with the number of students returning to campus in each county across the U.S. and (2) how different reopening policies at universities correlated with new COVID-19 cases.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Observational cohort study using publicly available data sources. Multivariable regression models estimated both effects of university reopening and different reopening policies.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Settings and Participants</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Populations in U.S. counties reporting new confirmed COVID-19 cases from August 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    to October 22
                    <jats:sup>nd</jats:sup>
                    .
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Exposures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>(1) total enrollment of students under the in-person or hybrid policies per county population and (2) proportion of online and hybrid enrollment within each county.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main Outcomes and Measures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Mean number of daily new confirmed COVID-19 cases per 10,000 county population from September 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    to October 22
                    <jats:sup>nd</jats:sup>
                    .
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    For 2,893 counties included in the study, mean number of daily confirmed cases per 10,000 county population rose from 1.51 from August 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    to August 31
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    to 1.98 from September 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    to October 22
                    <jats:sup>nd</jats:sup>
                    . Mean number of students returning to universities was 2.1% (95% CI, 1.8% to 2.3%) of the county population. The number of students returning to campus had an increased association (β = 2.006, P < 0.001) with new confirmed COVID-19 cases within the local county region where the institution resided. For 1,069 U.S. counties with universities, the mean proportion of online enrollment within each county was 40.1% (95% CI, 37.4% to 42.8%), with most students enrolling in-person or hybrid mode. In comparison to holding classes in-person, reopening universities online (β = -0.329, P < 0.001) or in a hybrid mode (β = -0.272, P = 0.012) had a decreased association with new confirmed COVID-19 cases.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions and Relevance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A higher number of students returning to campus in U.S. counties was associated with an increase in new confirmed COVID-19 cases; reopening online or partially online was associated with slower spread of the virus, in comparison to in-person reopening.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Key Points</jats:title>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Question</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Are students returning to universities and specific reopening policies associated with new confirmed coronavirus cases in United States?</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>In this cohort study of 2,893 U.S. counties, the number of students returning to campus was significantly associated with a higher number of new confirmed COVID-19 cases. In 1,069 U.S. counties with universities, online or hybrid reopening was significantly associated with a lower risk of new cases compared with in-person reopening.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Meaning</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>An increased risk of coronavirus infection was seen in surrounding regions after universities reopened last fall, and this effect was largest in those holding in-person classes.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                </jats:sec>
title: Association of University Reopening Policies with New Confirmed COVID-19 Cases in the United States

reference.unstructured: Hubler D , Hartocollis A. How Colleges Became the New Covid Hot Spots. The New York Times. https://www.nytimes.com/2020/09/11/us/college-campus-outbreak-covid.html. Publushed September 11, 2020. Accessed October 29, 2020.

Nierenberg A , Pasick A. A Crackdown in Ann Arbor. The New York Times. https://www.nytimes.com/2020/10/21/us/university-michigan-stay-in-place-order-testing.html. Published October, 21, 2020. Accessed October 29, 2020.

Here’s Our List of Colleges’ Reopening Models. The Chronicle of Higher Education https://www.chronicle.com/article/heres-a-list-of-colleges-plans-for-reopening-in-the-fall/. Accessed October 13, 2020.
C2i Dashboard. The College Crisis Initiative. https://collegecrisis.shinyapps.io/dashboard/. Accessed 16 October 2020.

County-level Data Sets. Economic Research Service: Department of Agriculture website. https://www.ers.usda.gov/data-products/county-level-data-sets/. Accessed October 16, 2020.

Tracking the Coronavirus at U.S Colleges and Universities. The New York Times. https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2020/us/covid-college-cases-tracker.html. Published August 26, 2020. Accessed January 26, 2021.
